text.title: semantic-field-naming Failure Modes
text.summary: Six antipatterns that cancel the accuracy lift of semantic renaming.

antipattern.symptom: Fields like `age_value`, `name_data`, `total_result` survive the rename pass.
antipattern.root-cause: The author treated the rename as "add a noun suffix" instead of "encode the meaning". `_value`/`_data` carry no information the type does not already convey.
antipattern.fix: Drop the suffix: `age_value` ‚Üí `age_years`, `name_data` ‚Üí `full_name`, `total_result` ‚Üí `total_revenue_usd`. Let the rest of the name carry the meaning.

antipattern.symptom: Output schema contains `field_a`, `field_b`, `field_c` or single-letter `x: int, y: int, n: int`.
antipattern.root-cause: Schema migrated from a CSV header or scratch prototype without semantic enrichment.
antipattern.fix: Replace each with what it actually represents: `x` ‚Üí `x_coordinate_pixels`, `n` ‚Üí `customer_count`, `field_a` ‚Üí the underlying domain noun.

antipattern.symptom: Field names mix English with another language: `montant_usd`, `total_eur`, `nazwa_klienta`.
antipattern.root-cause: Original codebase localised; the rename pass left the local-language token in place.
antipattern.fix: Pick one language ‚ÄĒ English has the strongest training-data prior. Translate the tokens: `montant_usd` ‚Üí `amount_usd`, `nazwa_klienta` ‚Üí `customer_name`. Preserve the original via `validation_alias` if an external API requires it.

antipattern.symptom: Renamed field uses `""` (empty string) or `-1` to mean "absent" instead of `None`.
antipattern.root-cause: Strict-mode bridges historically did not support `None`, so teams used sentinels. Modern bridges (Pydantic ‚Č•2.5, openai-python ‚Č•1.50) support `T | None`.
antipattern.fix: Use `field: T | None = None` and `ConfigDict(extra="forbid")` to keep the field required-with-nullable under strict mode. See [[strict-mode-required-fields]].

antipattern.symptom: `items: Item` (singular name for scalar field that actually holds a list) or `order: list[Order]` (singular name for list field).
antipattern.root-cause: Field name written before the type was finalised; type changed later but the name did not.
antipattern.fix: Sync name to cardinality every commit: singular for scalars, plural for lists, dict-prefixed for maps (`order_by_id`).

antipattern.symptom: Enum literals are `"OK"|"NOK"|"ERR"`, single letters, or numeric codes.
antipattern.root-cause: Schema mirrors a legacy system's error-code vocabulary the model has never seen.
antipattern.fix: Map to semantic vocabulary: `"OK"` ‚Üí `"success"`, `"NOK"` ‚Üí `"failure"`, `"ERR"` ‚Üí `"error"`. Keep the legacy code in a separate `legacy_code: str` field if downstream consumers need it.
